blocks.text: Carolina Exterior is Monroe's full-service outdoor living company, proudly serving homeowners across Union County and the greater Charlotte region. From routine lawn mowing to complete landscape transformations, we are the single company you call for every need your property has — and we show up, every time, exactly as scheduled. Our team is locally owned, licensed, and insured, with deep roots in the communities we serve and firsthand knowledge of North Carolina's soils, climate, and turf challenges.

At the heart of what we do is our Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract — the cornerstone service that takes the guesswork and hassle out of lawn care forever. Sign once, and we handle everything your property needs throughout the entire year: mowing, fertilization, weed control, pruning, leaf removal, and more. No scheduling headaches, no forgotten treatments, no scrambling to find different vendors for different tasks. Just a consistently beautiful lawn that makes your home the pride of the neighborhood.
Complete Residential Landscaping Services for Monroe & Union County Homeowners
Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract

Our Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract is the most comprehensive lawn care program available in Monroe and Union County. One contract covers mowing, edging, blowing, two applications of pre-emergent weed control, tall fescue weed management, four fertilizer applications, two insecticide treatments, flowerbed weed control, a yearly lime application, fall and winter leaf removal, and two rounds of bush and shrub pruning. This all-inclusive model means every aspect of your lawn's health is covered, scheduled, and executed without you lifting a finger.
Custom Landscaping Design & Installation

Carolina Exterior transforms outdoor spaces from concept to completion with custom landscape design and professional installation. Whether you are starting from scratch with a new home or refreshing an established yard, our team selects plants, shrubs, and groundcovers ideally suited to the Piedmont Carolina climate and your specific sun and soil conditions. We handle sod installation, seasonal color rotations, and complete bed creation and renovation to keep your property looking its best in every season.
Hardscape Patios, Walkways & Retaining Walls

Expand your living space beyond four walls with professionally installed hardscapes built to last decades. Carolina Exterior designs and installs patios, walkways, retaining walls, decorative borders, and steps using pavers, natural stone, concrete, and brick — materials chosen to match your home's architecture and your lifestyle. A well-designed hardscape increases property value, reduces maintenance burden, and turns your backyard into an outdoor retreat you actually use.
Mulching, Planting & Bed Care

Fresh mulch and professionally planted beds do more than look beautiful — they protect your soil, retain critical moisture during North Carolina's hot summers, and suppress weeds that compete with your plantings. Carolina Exterior installs brown, black, red, and natural hardwood mulch, plants seasonal flowers and ornamental grasses, and preps beds with proper edging and soil amendments. The result is a polished, healthy landscape that holds its appearance from one visit to the next.
Yard Drainage Solutions

Standing water, soggy turf, and hillside erosion are more than eyesores — they threaten your foundation, kill your grass, and create breeding grounds for mosquitoes. Carolina Exterior diagnoses and permanently corrects drainage problems across Union County's clay-heavy soils using French drains, yard grading, catch basins, downspout extensions, and swales. We find the root cause and engineer a lasting fix, not a temporary patch.
Why Monroe Homeowners Choose Carolina Exterior

One Company, Total Coverage: Rather than juggling a separate lawn crew, landscaper, and irrigation specialist, Carolina Exterior handles every outdoor service your property needs. Our annual contract clients enjoy the peace of mind that comes from a single point of contact who knows their property inside and out.

We Show Up as Scheduled: Reliability is not a bonus feature — it is our core promise. When your service day arrives, our crew arrives too. Monroe and Union County homeowners rely on us because we have earned that trust through consistent, on-time performance season after season.

Deep Local Knowledge: We work exclusively in the Carolinas. That means we understand the challenges of Piedmont clay soils, the timing requirements of tall fescue lawns, the pest pressures of humid Carolina summers, and the specific plant varieties that thrive in our region. Our recommendations are always grounded in real local experience.

Licensed, Insured & Professional: Carolina Exterior holds all required North Carolina licensing for landscape contracting and pesticide application. Every crew member is a trained professional, and every job is backed by full liability insurance. You can invite us onto your property with complete confidence.

Transparent Annual Pricing: Our annual maintenance contract clients pay a predictable, agreed-upon rate with no surprise invoices for standard services. Budgeting for lawn care becomes simple and stress-free, and you never have to wonder whether a treatment was skipped to save money.

What's Included in Our Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract
Our comprehensive annual contract covers every aspect of lawn health and curb appeal throughout the year:
Lawn Mowing: Scheduled mowing at the proper height for your grass type, including tall fescue maintained at 3.5 to 4 inches for optimal health and drought resistance.

Optional Add-On Services:
Aeration & Overseeding (Fall): Core aeration and tall fescue overseeding to thicken thin turf and improve long-term lawn density — the single most valuable fall service for Carolina lawns.

Fungus Treatments (2x/year available): Preventive and curative fungicide applications targeting brown patch, dollar spot, and other fungal diseases that thrive in North Carolina's humid summer climate.

blocks.text: Our Story

Carolina Exterior was born out of a simple conviction: homeowners in Monroe and Union County deserve a lawn care and landscaping company that is genuinely reliable, comprehensively skilled, and deeply rooted in the local community. We started as a small operation serving our neighbors in Monroe and quickly grew as word spread that we were the company that actually showed up — on time, every time — and did the work right. That reputation for dependability is something we guard fiercely, because we know it was earned one property at a time.

As we expanded across Union County into communities like Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, and beyond, we made a deliberate choice to stay focused on quality rather than growth for its own sake. We take on new clients when we are confident we can serve them at the level they expect and deserve. That approach has allowed us to build lasting relationships with homeowners across the region — many of our clients have been with us for years, trusting us with one of their largest investments: their home's exterior.

Today, Carolina Exterior serves homeowners and commercial properties from Monroe to Indian Land, from Weddington to Lancaster, with a full suite of outdoor services under one roof. We remain locally owned and operated, with a team that lives in the same communities we serve and takes genuine pride in making them more beautiful, one lawn at a time.
Our Mission

Our mission is straightforward: to be the most reliable, professional, and comprehensive outdoor services company in Monroe and Union County. We believe that every homeowner who invests in professional lawn care and landscaping deserves a company that treats their property with the same care and attention they would give it themselves. That means showing up when scheduled without exception, communicating clearly and proactively, using professional-grade materials and equipment, and standing behind every job we complete.

Reliability is not a selling point for us — it is a non-negotiable standard. When your service day arrives, our crew arrives. When we say your fertilizer application will happen in a certain week, it happens. When something unexpected arises on a property, we communicate it immediately rather than hoping you will not notice. This culture of accountability and follow-through is what sets Carolina Exterior apart in a crowded market.
What Makes Us Different

Full-Service, Single Vendor: From mowing and fertilization to landscape design and drainage engineering, Carolina Exterior handles it all. Our clients never need to coordinate between multiple companies or wonder which vendor is responsible for which service.

Annual Contract Model: Our maintenance contract clients get comprehensive, year-round care for one predictable annual rate. Every included service is scheduled automatically — no reminders, no missed treatments, no scrambling to book appointments each season.

Local NC Grass, Soil & Climate Expertise: We know what it takes to grow and maintain a beautiful lawn in Piedmont North Carolina. From managing the challenges of heavy clay soils to timing tall fescue seeding correctly, our local expertise produces results that out-of-area companies simply cannot match.

Licensed & Insured for Your Protection: Carolina Exterior holds all required North Carolina licensing for landscape contracting and pesticide application. Every job is covered by comprehensive liability insurance, giving our clients complete peace of mind every time our crew is on their property.

Responsive, Professional Communication: We understand that your time is valuable. We respond to inquiries promptly, communicate schedule changes proactively, and ensure you always know exactly what has been done on your property and what to expect next.
Our Licenses & Credentials

Carolina Exterior is fully licensed and insured for all services we provide in North Carolina and South Carolina. Our credentials include a North Carolina Landscape Contractor license and a Pesticide Applicator certification, which authorizes our team to legally and safely apply pre-emergent herbicides, post-emergent weed controls, fertilizers, insecticides, and fungicides on residential and commercial properties.

These credentials matter for a critical reason: many lawn care companies apply pesticides and herbicides without proper licensing, creating legal and safety risks for homeowners. When you hire Carolina Exterior, you know that every chemical application on your property is performed by certified applicators using registered products at legal, safe rates. That is the standard our clients deserve, and it is the standard we hold ourselves to without exception.
Serving the Communities We Call Home

Carolina Exterior is proud to be a genuine part of the communities we serve across Union County and beyond. Our team members live in Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, Monroe, and the surrounding areas — we drive the same roads our clients drive, our kids attend the same schools, and we take the same pride in our community's appearance that our clients do. When we maintain a lawn in Weddington or install a hardscape in Marvin, we are investing in our own neighborhood. That personal stake in the outcome is something that comes through in the quality and care of our work every single day. We also serve homeowners in Indian Land and Lancaster, South Carolina, extending the same community-focused approach across the state line.
Frequently Asked Questions

Yes. Carolina Exterior is fully licensed and insured for all services we provide. We hold a North Carolina Landscape Contractor license and a Pesticide Applicator certification, and we carry comprehensive liability insurance on every job. We are happy to provide documentation of our credentials and insurance coverage upon request.

Carolina Exterior has been serving Monroe and Union County for several years, growing steadily through word-of-mouth referrals from satisfied clients. We are a locally owned business with deep roots in the community, and our team includes experienced professionals with decades of combined expertise in lawn care, landscaping, and hardscape installation.

Our primary focus is residential service in Monroe and Union County, and the vast majority of our clients are homeowners. We do serve select commercial properties where our services are a good fit. Contact us to discuss your commercial property and we will let you know if we can meet your needs.

We respond to all inquiries within one business day and offer free property assessments so we can provide an accurate, customized quote. Our annual maintenance contract slots fill up each season, so we recommend reaching out early to secure your place on our schedule.

Whether you need a comprehensive annual lawn maintenance contract, a custom landscape installation, a new patio or retaining wall, or a permanent fix for a drainage problem, we have the expertise and the team to deliver results you will be proud of. residential-lawn-maintenance.h1: Annual Lawn Maintenance Contracts in Monroe, NC — Complete Year-Round Care
residential-lawn-maintenance.titleTag: Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract Monroe NC | Carolina Exterior
residential-lawn-maintenance.metaDescription: Carolina Exterior's annual lawn maintenance contract in Monroe NC covers mowing, fertilization, weed control, pruning & more. Year-round care for Union County lawns.

blocks.text: Most homeowners in Monroe and Union County have experienced the frustration of piecemeal lawn care: one company mows but does not fertilize, another handles weed control but forgets the timing, and by midsummer your lawn looks nothing like you hoped. Treatments get skipped, schedules slip, and the cumulative result is a yard that never reaches its potential. The problem is not a lack of effort — it is a lack of coordination, consistency, and expertise applied at exactly the right times throughout the year.

Carolina Exterior's Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract solves this problem completely. One contract, one company, one point of contact — and every service your lawn needs is scheduled and executed automatically, from the first pre-emergent application in late winter through the final leaf cleanup in December. Our contract clients do not think about their lawns because they do not have to. We handle everything, on time, every visit, for a predictable annual rate. This is what professional lawn care looks like in Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, and every other community we serve across Union County.
What's Included in Your Annual Lawn Maintenance Contract
Every service listed below is included in your annual contract at no additional charge. Our program is designed to cover every aspect of lawn health and appearance throughout the full calendar year.
Lawn Mowing

We mow your lawn on a consistent schedule throughout the growing season, adjusting frequency as growth dictates. For tall fescue — the dominant cool-season grass in Monroe and Union County — we maintain a cutting height of 3.5 to 4 inches, which is critical for root depth, drought tolerance, and natural weed suppression. Proper mowing height is one of the single most impactful factors in long-term lawn health, and our crews are trained to get it right every visit.
Edging

After every mowing visit, we edge all sidewalks, driveways, and planting bed borders to create clean, defined lines that dramatically improve your lawn's overall appearance. Consistent edging prevents grass from creeping into beds and hardscape areas and gives your property the maintained, professional look that distinguishes a truly cared-for lawn from one that is merely mowed.
Blowing

Every hard surface on your property — the driveway, sidewalks, walkways, and patios — is blown clear of grass clippings and debris after each service visit. This detail is often overlooked by budget lawn care operators, but it matters: clippings left on concrete stain and decompose, and a clean finish after each visit is a visible sign of professional-grade service.
Pre-Emergent Weed Control (2x/Year)

We apply pre-emergent herbicide twice annually — once in late winter to spring and again in early fall — to create a chemical barrier that prevents weed seeds from germinating. This two-application timing is specifically designed for North Carolina's climate, targeting crabgrass and other warm-season grassy weeds in spring and poa annua and winter annual broadleafs in fall. Consistent pre-emergent applications are the single most effective strategy for keeping a lawn weed-free without heavy reliance on post-emergent treatments.
Tall Fescue Weed Control

Tall fescue is North Carolina's most widely used cool-season turfgrass, and it faces specific weed pressures that require targeted management. Our program includes selective post-emergent herbicide applications to control broadleaf weeds like clover, henbit, chickweed, and wild violet, as well as grassy weeds that escape pre-emergent barriers. We use products registered and safe for use on tall fescue so we eliminate weeds without harming the turf.
Lawn Fertilization (4x/Year)

A four-application fertilization program timed to North Carolina's distinct seasons provides your lawn with the nutrients it needs at exactly the right growth stages. Our schedule includes a late-summer application to build root reserves before dormancy, a fall application to support active fescue growth, a late-fall application for root development through winter, and a spring application to fuel the season's first flush of growth. This approach is calibrated to North Carolina's climate and produces the deep green, dense turf that makes a property stand out.
Insecticide Treatments (2x/Year)

North Carolina's warm, humid climate supports a wide range of turf-damaging insects, and we apply two targeted insecticide treatments per year to keep them under control. Our program addresses grubs — the larvae of Japanese beetles and other scarab beetles that destroy root systems — as well as armyworms, which can devastate a lawn in days during late summer. Applications are timed to match the life cycles of the most destructive pests in our region.
Flowerbed Weed Control

Weeds in planting beds are a constant battle in North Carolina's growing climate, and our contract includes year-round flowerbed weed management. Our crews pull and treat weeds in all established planting beds during every service visit, keeping beds clean and professional-looking between seasonal color changes and mulch applications. Clean beds are as important to curb appeal as a well-mowed lawn, and we treat them with the same attention.
Lime Application (1x/Year)

Piedmont North Carolina's native soils are naturally acidic, and this acidity progressively reduces the effectiveness of fertilizer and harms turf health over time. We apply lime once per year to correct soil pH, bringing it into the optimal range for tall fescue and maximizing the return on your fertilization investment. A proper soil pH is foundational to every other aspect of lawn health — without it, even the best fertilizer program underperforms.
Leaf Removal

Our contract includes full leaf removal service in fall and winter to protect your turf through the dormant season. A heavy layer of leaves left on the lawn smothers the grass, encourages fungal disease, and creates ideal habitat for overwintering insects and voles. We clear your lawn completely, leaving nothing behind to damage your turf before spring growth begins.
Bush & Shrub Pruning (2x/Year)

We prune all shrubs and bushes on your property twice annually — once in spring after flowering and again in fall — to maintain healthy form, encourage dense branching, and keep your landscape looking polished and intentional. Our crews are trained in proper pruning techniques for the plant varieties common to Carolina landscapes, so your shrubs are shaped correctly, not just sheared indiscriminately.
Optional Add-On Services
Aeration & Overseeding (Fall)

Fall core aeration and tall fescue overseeding is the single most impactful optional service available to Union County homeowners with fescue lawns. Core aeration removes small plugs of soil to relieve compaction — a chronic problem in our region's heavy clay soils — improving water infiltration, root depth, and nutrient uptake. Overseeding immediately after aeration introduces fresh fescue seed into the prepared openings, thickening the lawn over time and replacing areas thinned by summer heat stress. We recommend this service every fall for all fescue lawns.
Fungus Treatments (2x/Year Available)

North Carolina's hot, humid summers create ideal conditions for turf fungal diseases, particularly brown patch and dollar spot in tall fescue lawns. Our optional fungicide treatment program includes up to two applications timed to the highest-risk periods of the season. For homeowners in shaded, low-airflow, or high-humidity areas — common in many Monroe and Waxhaw neighborhoods — fungicide treatments can be the difference between a healthy summer lawn and one that requires expensive fall renovation.
Why Choose an Annual Contract Over One-Time Services

Consistent Results Year-Round: Lawn health is cumulative. A pre-emergent applied at the wrong time is wasted money. Fertilizer applied without a soil pH correction is half as effective. Our annual contract ensures every treatment is timed correctly and works in concert with every other service for compounding results over time.

Priority Scheduling for Contract Clients: Annual contract clients receive priority placement on our schedule above one-time service requests. Your lawn gets serviced on time, every time, even during our busiest seasons. This is especially important for time-sensitive treatments like pre-emergent applications, where a one-week delay can mean the difference between a weed-free spring and a crabgrass problem.

Predictable Cost, No Surprise Invoices: One annual rate covers every included service. You know exactly what you are paying, and you never receive an unexpected invoice for a standard treatment. Budgeting for lawn care becomes simple and transparent.

Single-Vendor Convenience: One company, one phone number, one point of contact for every outdoor service your lawn needs. No coordinating between a separate mowing crew and a separate chemical company. No confusion about who is responsible for what. Carolina Exterior handles it all.
The Carolina Exterior Lawn Care Process

Get a Free Quote: Contact us for a free property assessment and no-obligation quote. We visit your property, assess your lawn's current condition, note any specific challenges or goals, and provide a detailed contract proposal.

Sign Your Annual Contract: Once you approve the proposal, we lock in your service schedule for the full year. You will know exactly which services are included, when they will be performed, and what your annual rate covers.

We Handle Everything: From the first pre-emergent application to the last leaf cleanup, our crew manages every service on your behalf. You receive notifications after each visit, and our team is always available to answer questions or address any concerns about your lawn.
Serving Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin & Surrounding Communities

Carolina Exterior provides annual lawn maintenance contracts to homeowners throughout Union County and the greater Charlotte region. Our service territory includes Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Wesley Chapel, Mineral Springs, Indian Trail, Indian Land, Lancaster, and the South Charlotte and Ballantyne neighborhoods of Mecklenburg County. Every community on this list receives the same level of professional service, consistent scheduling, and expert lawn care that Carolina Exterior is known for.

Whether your property is a half-acre lot in a Waxhaw HOA community, an estate home in Marvin, a newer construction in Indian Trail, or an established neighborhood in Monroe, our team has the local knowledge and the professional program to deliver outstanding results year-round.
Frequently Asked Questions
How often will you mow?

Mowing frequency follows the growth rate of your lawn throughout the season. During peak growing season in spring and early summer, we typically mow every week or every two weeks depending on growth rates. During slower periods — late summer heat stress and winter dormancy — mowing frequency adjusts accordingly. Our contract clients are never charged extra for mowing visits during heavy growth periods; the schedule is managed for your lawn's health, not for billing convenience.
When do pre-emergent treatments happen?

We apply pre-emergent herbicide twice per year, timed to North Carolina's specific weed germination windows. The first application goes down in late winter to early spring — typically February to March — targeting summer annual weeds like crabgrass. The second application is made in late summer to early fall — typically August to September — targeting winter annual weeds like poa annua. Timing these applications correctly is critical; a pre-emergent applied too late provides no benefit, which is why we manage the schedule for you.
What grass types do you service?

Carolina Exterior services all grass types common to the Monroe and Union County area, including tall fescue, Bermuda, Zoysia, and centipede. The majority of lawns in our service area are tall fescue, and our standard program is optimized for this grass. For Bermuda, Zoysia, or centipede lawns, we adjust mowing heights, fertilization schedules, and weed control products accordingly. We identify your grass type during the initial property assessment and customize the program to match.
Can I add services mid-contract?

Yes. Contract clients can add optional services like aeration and overseeding or fungicide treatments at any point during the contract year. We also offer landscaping, mulching, hardscape, and drainage services that can be scheduled separately from your maintenance contract. All add-on services are priced transparently, and our team will always discuss the recommendation and cost before scheduling any additional work.
Get Your Free Lawn Maintenance Contract Quote Today

Carolina Exterior is currently accepting new annual maintenance contract clients across Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, and the surrounding Union County area. Annual contract slots are limited and fill up each season, so we recommend reaching out as early as possible to secure your property's spot on our schedule. Contact us at (704) 975-5867 or visit CarolinaExteriorLandscapes.com to request your free property assessment and quote. Let us show you what a truly complete, professionally managed lawn care program looks like.

residential-landscaping.h1: Residential Landscaping Services in Monroe, NC — Design, Installation & More
residential-landscaping.titleTag: Residential Landscaping Monroe NC | Design & Installation
residential-landscaping.metaDescription: Carolina Exterior provides custom residential landscaping in Monroe NC -- design, plant installation, sod, seasonal color & bed renovation. Free estimates for Union County.

blocks.text: Your outdoor space is an extension of your home — and it deserves the same thoughtful design and quality craftsmanship you would expect indoors. Carolina Exterior provides complete residential landscaping services for homeowners across Monroe and Union County, from initial concept and design through full installation and optional ongoing care. Whether you are transforming a bare builder-grade yard, refreshing an overgrown landscape, or adding seasonal color to an already beautiful property, our team has the plant knowledge, design eye, and installation expertise to bring your vision to life.

What sets Carolina Exterior apart in Monroe's competitive landscaping market is our local depth of knowledge. We work exclusively in the Carolinas, which means we know exactly which plants thrive in Union County's clay soils and humid subtropical climate, which grass varieties establish best in our region, and which designs hold up beautifully through both the blazing heat of a Carolinas summer and the occasional hard freeze of a Piedmont winter. Our recommendations are practical, proven, and tailored to your specific property — never a generic catalog selection pasted into a new location.
Our Residential Landscaping Services
Custom Landscape Design

Every great landscape starts with a thoughtful plan. Our design process begins with a thorough site assessment — we evaluate your soil conditions, sun and shade patterns, drainage characteristics, existing plants, and your home's architectural style. From there, we develop a customized landscape plan that balances visual impact, seasonal interest, plant health, and long-term maintainability. We present the design for your review and incorporate your feedback before a single plant goes in the ground. Whether you want a formal, manicured look or a naturalistic, layered planting, we design landscapes that reflect your personal taste and complement your home.
Plant & Shrub Installation

We source and install quality plants that are well-suited to the Piedmont Carolina climate and your property's specific conditions. Our plant selection prioritizes species with proven track records in Union County's soil and climate — varieties that establish readily, require reasonable maintenance, and perform beautifully through our region's wide seasonal range. We install deciduous and evergreen shrubs, ornamental trees, perennials, groundcovers, and ornamental grasses, all planted at proper depth with appropriate soil amendments to give each plant the best possible start.
Sod Installation

Sod installation is the fastest path to a full, established lawn — and Carolina Exterior handles every aspect of the process from site preparation through final installation. We grade and prepare the soil surface, select the right grass variety for your yard's conditions, install sod in tight, interlocking rows, and provide guidance on the critical establishment watering regimen. For Monroe and Union County lawns, we commonly install tall fescue sod for shaded and mixed-exposure yards, as well as Bermuda and Zoysia for high-sun applications where warm-season grasses excel.
Seasonal Color Programs

Year-round curb appeal requires more than permanent plantings — it requires a rotating cast of seasonal annuals and flowering plants that bring color and life to your landscape in every season. blocks.text: Your outdoor living space should be as functional and beautiful as the interior of your home — and professionally installed hardscape is the foundation that makes that possible. A well-executed hardscape project does not just improve your daily outdoor experience — it measurably increases your property's value and reduces long-term landscape maintenance requirements.

Union County homeowners enjoy some of the most beautiful residential properties in the greater Charlotte region, and outdoor living is central to that lifestyle. From the large estate lots of Marvin and Weddington to the newer subdivisions of Indian Trail and Wesley Chapel, Carolina Exterior brings the same level of craftsmanship and attention to detail to every hardscape project. Our Hardscape Services
Patio Installation

Walkways & Pathways

A thoughtfully designed walkway does far more than connect two points on your property — it guides guests safely, defines the landscape, creates visual structure, and adds substantial curb appeal. Carolina Exterior installs front entry walkways, garden pathways, side-yard connections, and pool surrounds using pavers, natural stone, brick, and concrete. We design walkways with appropriate width, gentle curves that follow the natural flow of movement, and materials that complement your home's exterior and your existing landscape design. A new front walkway is one of the highest-ROI curb appeal investments a Monroe or Union County homeowner can make.
Retaining Walls

Retaining walls serve a dual purpose in Carolina landscapes: they provide critical structural support for sloped terrain and erosion control, and they are a powerful design element that adds visual interest, defined planting levels, and architectural character to the landscape. Carolina Exterior builds retaining walls from natural stone, concrete block, and brick, engineered to handle the soil pressure and drainage demands of your specific site. Union County's varied topography — from the rolling hills of Weddington and Marvin to flatter terrain in Indian Trail — creates frequent opportunities where a properly built retaining wall transforms a challenging slope into a beautiful, usable outdoor space.
Decorative Borders & Edging

Decorative hardscape borders define the boundaries between lawn, planting beds, driveways, and walkways with a clean, permanent edge that outlasts plastic or aluminum edging by decades. Carolina Exterior installs paver, brick, and natural stone borders that add a finished, polished look to every transition on your property. A decorative border around the perimeter of a driveway, along a planting bed, or framing a patio creates visual cohesion that ties the entire landscape together.
Steps & Stairs

Sloped yards and grade changes are common throughout Union County's hilly terrain, and professionally built steps are the safe, beautiful solution for navigating those elevation changes. Carolina Exterior constructs steps and stairs from natural stone, pavers, and concrete block — materials chosen to match the style of your existing hardscape or home exterior. We build steps with proper rise and run dimensions for comfortable, safe use and finish each project with a clean, integrated look that makes the steps feel like a natural part of the landscape rather than an afterthought.
Hardscape Materials We Work With

Pavers: Concrete pavers are the most popular hardscape material in Union County for good reason — they are durable, frost-resistant, available in a wide range of colors and textures, and individually replaceable. Ideal for patios, walkways, driveways, and decorative borders.

Natural Stone: Flagstone, bluestone, slate, and granite bring a timeless, high-end aesthetic that commands attention. Natural stone is ideal for patios, steps, and garden pathways where an organic, luxurious look is the priority.

Concrete: Poured concrete is a practical, cost-effective option for large patios, walkways, and stepping stones. Modern decorative concrete techniques including stamping and coloring can achieve sophisticated looks at a lower cost than natural stone.

Brick: Traditional brick hardscape has a warm, classic appeal that complements many of the established architectural styles found in Monroe and Waxhaw neighborhoods. Brick is durable, low-maintenance, and ages gracefully over time.

The Value of Hardscaping in Monroe & Union County

Hardscape improvements consistently rank among the highest-return home improvement investments. For homeowners in Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, and Marvin — where property values are strong and buyers expect elevated outdoor living spaces — hardscape quality can be a significant factor in a home's market appeal.

Beyond resale value, there is the day-to-day quality of life improvement that comes from having a beautiful, functional outdoor space. Our Hardscape Installation Process

How long does a patio installation take?

mulching-and-planting.h1: Professional Mulching & Planting Services in Monroe, NC

mulching-and-planting.metaDescription: Professional mulching and planting services in Monroe NC. Brown, black, red & natural mulch installation, seasonal flowers, shrubs & bed prep across Union County.

blocks.text: There is a clear difference between a yard that looks maintained and one that looks truly cared for — and fresh, professionally installed mulch combined with thoughtful plantings is what creates that distinction. Carolina Exterior provides comprehensive mulching and planting services for homeowners across Monroe and Union County, handling everything from bulk mulch installation to seasonal flower programs to complete bed preparation and renovation. Whether your beds need a fresh coat of mulch heading into spring, a full planting overhaul, or a year-round rotating seasonal color program, our team delivers the results that make your property stand out on the street.

Mulching and planting might seem like simple services, but done correctly they require real knowledge: the right mulch depth, the right plant selection for your soil and sun conditions, proper bed edging, and an understanding of how plantings will mature over time. Carolina Exterior brings that expertise to every project across Marvin, Waxhaw, Weddington, Indian Trail, Monroe, and the surrounding communities of Union County.
Mulching Services
Types of Mulch We Install
Carolina Exterior installs four primary types of mulch, each with distinct visual and functional characteristics:

Brown Hardwood Mulch: The most popular choice throughout Union County neighborhoods, brown hardwood mulch provides a warm, natural appearance that complements virtually any home exterior and landscape design. It breaks down over time, adding organic matter to the soil and improving long-term soil health.

Black Dyed Mulch: Black mulch creates a dramatic, high-contrast backdrop that makes green foliage and colorful flowers pop visually. It holds its color longer than natural mulch due to the dye treatment, making it a popular choice for homeowners who prioritize a consistent, manicured appearance between applications.

Red Dyed Mulch: Red mulch adds bold visual warmth and works especially well with brick and stone home exteriors. Like black mulch, the dye treatment extends color retention, and the earthy red tone creates a distinctive, eye-catching landscape aesthetic.

Natural/Undyed Hardwood Mulch: For homeowners who prefer a completely organic appearance or who have specific concerns about dyed mulches near edible gardens, natural undyed hardwood mulch provides all the functional benefits of mulch — moisture retention, weed suppression, soil temperature regulation — without colorants. It weathers to a soft gray-brown tone that blends naturally into the landscape.
Benefits of Professional Mulching
Properly installed mulch delivers multiple benefits beyond curb appeal that directly affect the health and long-term cost of your landscape:

Moisture Retention: In North Carolina's hot summers, mulch can reduce soil moisture loss by up to 70%, dramatically reducing the irrigation requirements of your planting beds and keeping shallow-rooted annuals alive during dry stretches.

Weed Suppression: A properly installed 2 to 3 inch layer of mulch blocks sunlight to the soil surface, suppressing weed seed germination and significantly reducing the hand-weeding and herbicide treatment your beds require throughout the season.

Soil Temperature Regulation: Mulch insulates soil against temperature extremes — keeping roots cooler in summer heat and protecting them from freeze damage during winter cold snaps. This is particularly valuable for the ornamental plants and perennials common in Union County landscapes.

Curb Appeal: Fresh mulch is one of the fastest, highest-impact curb appeal improvements available. Clean, uniformly mulched beds with sharp edges signal a well-maintained property and create a polished frame for your plantings and home exterior.
How Much Mulch Do You Need?

The correct mulch depth is 2 to 3 inches — enough to deliver weed suppression and moisture retention benefits without smothering plant roots. To calculate cubic yards needed, multiply the bed length by width by the desired depth in feet, then divide by 27. Most residential properties require between 3 and 10 cubic yards of mulch per application depending on the total bed area.

One critical mulching mistake to avoid is volcano mulching — piling mulch in a cone shape directly against tree trunks and shrub stems. This practice traps moisture against the bark, promotes fungal rot, and can kill otherwise healthy trees over time. Carolina Exterior's crews are trained to keep mulch pulled back 2 to 4 inches from all trunks and stems, maintaining the flat, even surface that is both visually correct and biologically sound.
Planting & Bed Services
Seasonal Flower Planting

A rotating seasonal color program keeps your property vibrant and welcoming in every season of the year. Carolina Exterior plants spring annuals — pansies, snapdragons, petunias, and lobelia — as temperatures warm in February and March, providing immediate color through the spring flush. Summer plantings introduce heat-tolerant varieties like vinca, lantana, marigolds, and pentas that thrive through Carolina's intense July and August heat. Fall brings the warm tones of mums, ornamental kale, ornamental peppers, and cool-season annuals that carry the landscape through November. We manage color transitions seamlessly so your beds are never dull or bare between seasons.
Shrub & Ornamental Grass Installation

Selecting the right shrubs and ornamental grasses for a specific site requires knowledge of mature size, sun and moisture requirements, deer pressure, and aesthetic compatibility with the surrounding plantings. Carolina Exterior selects and installs shrubs and ornamental grasses well-suited to the specific conditions of your property — sun-drenched front beds, shaded side yards, or the transitional areas that get partial light throughout the day. We favor proven performers in Union County's clay soils, including Loropetalum, Knockout roses, ornamental grasses like Miscanthus and Karl Foerster, Indian hawthorn, and hollies — plants that establish readily and deliver strong visual returns.
Bed Preparation & Cleanup

New beds and renovated beds require proper foundation work before a single plant goes in the ground. Carolina Exterior prepares planting beds with clean edge cuts that define a sharp boundary between bed and lawn, weed removal and optional pre-emergent treatment, soil amendment with compost or topsoil where needed, and weed barrier installation where appropriate. For existing beds in need of seasonal cleanup — overgrown perennials, winter-damaged plants, accumulated debris — we perform a thorough cleanup that removes the old growth and prepares the beds for a fresh season.
Spring & Fall Mulching Programs

Twice-yearly mulching is the optimal schedule for most properties in the Monroe and Union County area. Spring mulch — installed in March and April after the last frost — refreshes beds for the high-visibility growing season, when your property gets the most attention from neighbors, visitors, and potential buyers. Fall mulch — installed in October and November — provides critical insulation for plant roots heading into winter and gives your property a clean, finished look through the otherwise drab late-season months.

Carolina Exterior offers scheduled spring and fall mulching programs for existing clients, making it easy to ensure your beds are always looking their best at the most important times of year. For annual maintenance contract clients, mulch scheduling can be coordinated with your regular service calendar for seamless property management.
Serving Monroe, Indian Trail, Waxhaw & Surrounding Communities

Carolina Exterior provides mulching and planting services to homeowners throughout our full service territory. We regularly serve clients in Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, Wesley Chapel, Mineral Springs, Indian Land, Lancaster, and the South Charlotte area. Our team is familiar with the soil conditions, sun patterns, HOA requirements, and aesthetic preferences common in each of these communities, which allows us to make plant selections and design recommendations that are truly right for your specific property and neighborhood.
Frequently Asked Questions
How often should I replace mulch?

Most residential properties benefit from fresh mulch once or twice per year. Mulch breaks down naturally over time — typically fading in color and compressing in depth within 6 to 12 months depending on the type. Annual mulching in spring maintains consistent appearance and ensures beds always have the correct functional depth. Properties with high aesthetic standards or active HOA oversight often benefit from a second fall application to keep beds looking sharp year-round.
What type of mulch is best for NC?

Brown hardwood mulch is the most widely used and practical choice for most Union County and Monroe-area properties. It provides excellent functional benefits, has a natural appearance that complements most home styles, and is readily available locally. Black dyed mulch is the best option for homeowners who prioritize consistent, long-lasting color contrast against green plantings. The right choice depends on your home's exterior color, your landscape design aesthetic, and your personal preference — our team is happy to make a specific recommendation based on your property.
When is the best time to plant shrubs in Monroe NC?

Fall — September through November — is the ideal time to plant shrubs and ornamental trees in Monroe and Union County. Cooler temperatures reduce transplant stress, and fall rain patterns help establish root systems before winter dormancy. Spring planting from March through May is also excellent. Summer planting is possible but requires more diligent irrigation to get new plants through the heat. We avoid planting during the hottest weeks of July and August whenever possible.
Do you remove old mulch before installing new?

For most applications, we install new mulch directly over existing mulch without full removal, provided the existing layer is not so deep that adding more would create excessive depth. The industry standard is to maintain a total depth of 2 to 3 inches. If existing mulch is excessively deep, compacted, or showing signs of mold or pest activity, we remove and dispose of the old material before installing fresh mulch. We assess each bed individually and let you know our recommendation before proceeding.
Schedule Your Mulching & Planting Service Today

Carolina Exterior schedules mulching and planting services throughout our service territory in Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, and the surrounding Union County communities. Spring mulching slots book quickly in February and March — contact us early to secure your preferred install date. Call (704) 975-5867 or visit CarolinaExteriorLandscapes.com to request a free estimate. We respond within one business day and provide transparent, itemized quotes for all mulching and planting services.

drainage-solutions.h1: Yard Drainage Solutions in Monroe, NC — Fix Standing Water & Erosion
drainage-solutions.titleTag: Yard Drainage Solutions Monroe NC | Fix Standing Water
drainage-solutions.metaDescription: Carolina Exterior fixes standing water, soggy lawns & erosion in Monroe NC. French drains, yard grading, catch basins & downspout extensions for Union County homeowners.
secondaryKeywords: standing water lawn fix Monroe NC

blocks.text: Standing water in your yard does not just look bad — it is actively damaging your property. Every time water pools in low spots and sits for more than a few hours after rain, it is suffocating grass roots, promoting fungal disease, creating mosquito breeding habitat, saturating your soil structure, and potentially threatening your home's foundation. In a region like Union County, where heavy clay soils dramatically slow drainage and intense summer thunderstorms can dump inches of rain in an hour, drainage problems are extremely common — and they do not fix themselves.

Carolina Exterior diagnoses and permanently corrects yard drainage problems for homeowners across Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, and the surrounding Union County communities. We do not apply temporary fixes — we assess the true root cause of the problem, design the appropriate engineered solution, and install it correctly so you stop worrying about rain and start enjoying your outdoor space. Our drainage expertise is built on real, hands-on experience with the specific soil conditions, slope characteristics, and rainfall patterns of Piedmont North Carolina.
Common Yard Drainage Problems We Solve
Standing Water After Rain: Water that pools in the yard and remains visible 24 or more hours after a rain event, indicating soil saturation or a low-point with no outlet.

Soggy, Spongy Turf: Lawn areas that remain perpetually soft and muddy even during dry periods, signaling chronic drainage failure and potentially compacted or clay-dominated soil that cannot move water.

Foundation and Basement Water Intrusion: Water draining toward the home rather than away from it, pooling against the foundation, and potentially entering basements or crawl spaces during heavy rain events.
Hillside and Slope Erosion: Sloped areas where rainwater accelerates and strips topsoil, leaving exposed roots, bare channels, and sediment deposits at the base of the slope.
Downspout Flooding: Gutters and downspouts that discharge directly against the house or into an area with no drainage pathway, creating localized flooding and foundation saturation.
Waterlogged Flower Beds: Planting beds that hold water and remain saturated after rain, creating root rot conditions for shrubs, perennials, and annuals that cannot tolerate standing water.
Our Drainage Solutions
French Drain Installation

A French drain is one of the most effective and widely applicable drainage solutions for residential properties in Union County. The system consists of a perforated pipe wrapped in filter fabric, buried in a gravel-filled trench that intercepts groundwater and subsurface flow, and directs it away from the problem area to a safe outlet — a storm drain, a natural waterway, or a daylight outlet at the property edge. French drains work particularly well for properties with chronic wet areas caused by high groundwater, slope drainage accumulation, or areas adjacent to impervious surfaces. We install French drains throughout our service territory from Waxhaw to Indian Land, properly sizing the pipe, gravel, and outlet for each specific site's drainage volume and slope.
Yard Regrading & Grading

Many drainage problems are fundamentally a grading issue — the land slopes toward the house rather than away from it, or low spots have developed over time through soil settlement and erosion. Yard regrading corrects the slope of the ground surface so that water naturally flows away from structures and toward appropriate outlets. This is often the most direct and cost-effective solution for foundation drainage problems and broad wet areas that result from incorrect grading. Carolina Exterior grades residential properties of all sizes, from small targeted corrections around a foundation to full-yard regrading on newly constructed or recently disturbed sites.
Catch Basin Installation

A catch basin is an in-ground inlet that collects surface water at specific problem points — the low corner of a yard, the base of a downspout, or a hard surface where runoff concentrates — and connects to underground pipe that carries the water to a safe outlet. Catch basins are often the right solution when water is concentrating at a specific point rather than coming from a broad subsurface source. We design and install catch basin systems sized appropriately for the drainage volume of each site, using durable HDPE or concrete structures that hold up through decades of use.
Downspout Extensions & Management

Improperly terminated downspouts are one of the most common causes of foundation water problems in Monroe and Union County residential properties. When a downspout discharges directly against the foundation or into a splash block that does not move water far enough from the house, every rain event saturates the soil immediately adjacent to the foundation wall. Carolina Exterior installs underground downspout extensions that carry roof runoff safely away from the structure, daylight it at an appropriate point in the yard, and eliminate the foundation saturation cycle permanently. This is often one of the most impactful and cost-effective drainage improvements a homeowner can make.
Swales

A swale is a gently sloped, grass-lined channel that moves surface water safely across a property from one point to another without the need for underground pipe. Swales are a natural, low-cost drainage solution well-suited to larger lots where water needs to be redirected across significant distances. Carolina Exterior designs and installs swales that integrate naturally into the landscape, blending with the surrounding turf while providing a functional drainage pathway that handles normal rainfall volumes without erosion or flooding.
Our Drainage Assessment Process

Site Visit & Assessment: We visit your property during or after a rain event when possible, or assess existing drainage patterns, soil conditions, and landscape topography to diagnose the true source of your drainage problem. We do not guess — we systematically evaluate every factor that contributes to the issue.

Diagnosis & Solution Design: Based on our assessment, we identify the root cause of the drainage failure and design the appropriate corrective solution. We explain the problem clearly, describe the proposed solution in plain language, and provide options where multiple approaches could work.

Solution Proposal & Installation: We provide a written proposal with clear scope, materials, and pricing. Once approved, our crew completes the installation efficiently and thoroughly, restoring any disturbed turf or landscape areas as part of the project closeout.
Why Drainage Problems Must Be Fixed

Ignoring a yard drainage problem is never the right choice — the consequences compound over time. Chronically wet soil accelerates lawn disease, kills turf, and creates conditions that require expensive renovation. Foundation water intrusion leads to structural damage, mold growth, and significantly reduced property value. Slope erosion removes topsoil that takes decades to replace naturally. Mosquito populations thrive in standing water, turning an unusable yard into an unusable outdoor space for an entirely different reason.

Addressing drainage problems proactively — before the damage is done — is almost always less expensive than repairing the consequences. A properly designed and installed drainage system solves the problem permanently, protects your home and landscape investment, and often unlocks areas of your property that were previously unusable. Carolina Exterior has helped homeowners throughout Waxhaw, Monroe, Weddington, and Indian Trail reclaim their yards and protect their homes with solutions that work in North Carolina's specific soil and climate conditions.
Serving Waxhaw, Weddington, Monroe & Surrounding Union County

Carolina Exterior provides yard drainage solutions throughout Union County and the greater Charlotte region. Drainage project clients include homeowners in Monroe, Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, Wesley Chapel, Mineral Springs, Indian Land, Lancaster, and the South Charlotte neighborhoods of Mecklenburg County. Union County's clay-heavy soils and varied topography create drainage challenges across the region, and our team has the local experience to diagnose and solve them correctly. We understand the soil conditions, slope patterns, and rainfall dynamics of Piedmont North Carolina, and that local knowledge is the foundation of every drainage solution we design.
Frequently Asked Questions
How do I know if I need a French drain?

A French drain is likely the right solution if you have a persistent wet area in your yard that receives drainage from a broad area — a slope, a neighboring property, or a large paved surface. Signs include a low spot that holds water for more than 24 hours after rain, a consistently soggy strip of turf along a fence or property line, or basement moisture that correlates with rain events. During our site assessment, we evaluate whether a French drain, regrading, catch basin, or another solution is the best fit for your specific conditions.
How much does yard drainage cost in NC?

Drainage project costs vary widely based on scope, solution type, and site conditions. A simple downspout extension installation may cost a few hundred dollars, while a comprehensive French drain system with multiple inlets and a long outlet pipe can range from $2,000 to $8,000 or more depending on length and complexity. Yard regrading projects are priced by the scope of grading required. Carolina Exterior provides free, detailed written estimates for every drainage project so you know exactly what you are paying for and why before any work begins.
Will fixing drainage kill my existing grass?

Drainage installation does require trenching and excavation that disturbs existing turf in the work area. Carolina Exterior minimizes surface disruption wherever possible and restores disturbed areas with seed, sod, or topsoil as part of the project closeout. In most cases, the lawn fully recovers within one growing season after drainage installation — and because the chronic wet conditions are eliminated, the turf in and around the previously problematic area grows significantly better after the fix than it did before.
Can you fix drainage and do landscaping in the same project?

Yes, and we frequently recommend coordinating both. Drainage corrections often require grading and soil work that is an ideal starting point for landscape installation or renovation — once the grade is corrected and the drainage system is in place, fresh topsoil, new plantings, and fresh mulch transform the corrected area into a finished, attractive outdoor space rather than a bare construction site. Combining drainage and landscaping in a single project reduces total cost and delivers a complete result in one mobilization.

blocks.text: The best commercial grounds maintenance program is one you never have to think about. Carolina Exterior provides structured, scheduled grounds management programs for commercial properties across Monroe, Indian Trail, Waxhaw, and the greater Union County area. Our crews arrive on time, complete every service item on your scope of work, and leave your property looking sharp without requiring constant follow-up from your property management team.

Whether you manage a single office building, a multi-tenant retail strip, an HOA community, or an industrial campus, Carolina Exterior builds a customized grounds maintenance program around your property's specific turf types, plantings, hardscape features, and seasonal needs. We operate on documented schedules, communicate service completions, and handle the full scope of exterior maintenance so your tenants and customers always experience a well-kept property.
What's Included in Our Commercial Grounds Maintenance Program
Mowing & Turf Management

Healthy, professional-looking turf starts with proper mowing practices. Carolina Exterior mows commercial properties at the correct height for each grass type — typically Bermuda, Zoysia, or Fescue in the Monroe and Union County area — using commercial-grade equipment calibrated for the scale of your property. We offer weekly, bi-weekly, and custom frequency schedules based on your turf's growth rate and seasonal conditions. All mowing includes string trimming around structures, signs, light poles, trees, and any obstacle the mower cannot reach.
Edging Along Curbs, Sidewalks & Hardscapes

Clean edges are one of the clearest signals of a professionally maintained property. Carolina Exterior provides mechanical edging along all curbs, sidewalks, parking lot islands, and bed lines on every scheduled service visit. Sharp, defined edges keep turf contained, prevent encroachment into paved areas, and create the crisp visual line that distinguishes a well-maintained commercial property.
Blowing & Hard Surface Cleanup

Every service visit concludes with a thorough blowdown of all paved surfaces including sidewalks, parking lots, entry areas, and any other hardscape on the property. We remove clippings, debris, and organic material from all paved areas so your property is clean and professional immediately following our visit.
Turf & Bed Weed Control

Weed pressure in turf and landscape beds is one of the most visible maintenance failures on commercial properties. Carolina Exterior includes both turf and bed weed control in comprehensive grounds maintenance contracts. We apply pre-emergent and post-emergent treatments on appropriate schedules, targeting broadleaf and grassy weed populations in turf areas and keeping landscape beds clean and defined.
Fertilization Programs

Consistent fertilization is what separates lush, healthy commercial turf from thin, stressed grass that browns out under summer heat or goes dormant early in fall. Carolina Exterior provides seasonal fertilization programs appropriate for Union County's climate and your property's specific turf type, typically including spring, summer, and fall winterizer applications.
Shrub & Hedge Trimming

Overgrown shrubs and hedges make even a well-mowed property look neglected. Carolina Exterior includes shrub and hedge trimming in comprehensive maintenance contracts, shaping all foundation plantings, ornamental shrubs, and formal hedges on a seasonal schedule. We trim to appropriate natural forms and sizes, removing dead wood and maintaining plantings at proportions that complement your architecture.
Seasonal Leaf Removal & Cleanup

Leaf accumulation in fall and winter creates turf damage, clogged storm drains, slippery walkways, and a disheveled appearance. Carolina Exterior provides scheduled leaf removal for commercial properties throughout the fall season and spring cleanups to prepare turf and beds for the growing season.
Commercial Contract Options
Carolina Exterior offers three contract structures for commercial grounds maintenance, designed to match different property types and budget cycles:

Annual Contracts: Full-year service agreements covering all scheduled maintenance visits, seasonal programs, and cleanup services. Annual contracts provide the lowest per-visit cost, priority scheduling, and predictable annual budgeting. Recommended for properties requiring consistent year-round appearance.

Seasonal Contracts: Growing-season contracts typically covering April through November in Union County. Ideal for properties with reduced winter maintenance needs or that handle off-season cleanup internally.

Monthly Contracts: Month-to-month agreements that provide scheduling consistency without a long-term commitment. Offer flexibility for properties with uncertain long-term plans or new management teams evaluating service providers.
Multi-Site Property Management

Carolina Exterior is equipped to manage grounds maintenance across multiple commercial locations under a single service agreement. For property management companies and multi-location business operators, we offer consolidated contracts that standardize service specifications across all sites, assign dedicated account management, coordinate crew scheduling for geographic efficiency, and consolidate all invoicing into a single monthly or quarterly statement.

Whether you manage two locations or twenty across the Monroe and Charlotte region, multi-site agreements with Carolina Exterior eliminate the administrative overhead of managing separate vendor relationships for each property and ensure consistent service standards regardless of which site you visit.

Commercial Hardscape Services
Parking Lot Islands & Borders

Parking lot islands define traffic flow, provide landscaping opportunities within paved areas, and reduce pavement heat islands on commercial properties. Carolina Exterior installs commercial walkways in concrete pavers and natural stone with proper subbase preparation, appropriate joint widths, cross-slope grading for drainage, and transitions that meet ADA requirements at all entry points and changes in grade.
Retaining Walls for Commercial Sites

Carolina Exterior installs engineered block retaining walls for commercial sites that manage slope transitions, prevent soil movement, create usable grade changes, and provide attractive, durable site features. Carolina Exterior specifies and installs commercial hardscape materials selected for durability, aesthetics, and long-term performance:

Concrete Pavers: The most versatile commercial hardscape material. commercial-drainage.h1: Commercial Drainage & Site Work in Monroe, NC — Solve Flooding, Erosion & Runoff
commercial-drainage.titleTag: Commercial Drainage Solutions Monroe NC | Carolina Exterior
commercial-drainage.metaDescription: Fix parking lot flooding, erosion & stormwater runoff on your commercial property. Carolina Exterior provides drainage solutions across Monroe, Indian Trail & Union County NC.
secondaryKeywords: commercial site drainage Union County NC

blocks.text: Drainage problems on commercial properties are more than an inconvenience — they cause structural damage, create slip-and-fall liability, drive tenant complaints, and in some cases trigger stormwater management compliance issues. A parking lot that floods after every rain, a green space that stays waterlogged for days, or a foundation consistently receiving water runoff all represent real financial and legal exposure for property owners and managers.

Carolina Exterior diagnoses and resolves commercial drainage problems for businesses, HOAs, and commercial property owners across Union County and the greater Charlotte region. We assess drainage patterns, identify root causes rather than just symptoms, and install engineered solutions that manage water correctly for the long term. From French drain systems and catch basins to full site regrading and stormwater management, our commercial drainage team handles projects of all scales.
Commercial Drainage Problems We Solve
Carolina Exterior routinely addresses the following drainage challenges on commercial properties across the Monroe and Union County area:
Parking lot flooding: Standing water following rain events, creating hazardous conditions for pedestrians and vehicles and potential damage to paved surfaces over time.
Green space ponding: Turf and landscape areas that hold water for extended periods, killing vegetation and preventing normal property use.
Foundation drainage issues: Water accumulating against building foundations due to improper grading, insufficient drainage capacity, or failed existing drainage infrastructure.
Slope and embankment erosion: Unprotected slopes losing topsoil and creating sediment problems in drainage systems and adjacent properties.
Downspout overflow: Building roof drainage discharging in volumes that exceed the capacity of existing ground-level drainage systems, causing localized saturation.
Stormwater runoff compliance: Properties receiving notice of stormwater management deficiencies from local municipalities or property management covenants requiring documented corrective action.
Our Commercial Drainage Services
French Drain Systems for Commercial Sites

French drain systems are one of the most effective and durable solutions for managing subsurface water movement and surface drainage across commercial properties. Carolina Exterior designs and installs commercial-scale French drain systems using perforated pipe, appropriate aggregate, and filter fabric sized for the drainage volume requirements of your site. Commercial French drain installations are engineered to handle the runoff volumes generated by large paved areas, buildings, and green spaces, routing collected water to appropriate discharge points away from structures and paved surfaces.
Catch Basin Installation & Maintenance

Catch basins collect surface water at specific low points on commercial properties and route it through underground piping to discharge locations. They are the standard solution for parking lot flooding, low spots in paved areas, and areas where surface drainage cannot be managed through grading alone. Carolina Exterior installs new catch basins with properly sized grates, connected piping, and appropriate outlets, and rehabilitates or replaces deteriorated existing catch basin infrastructure.
Site Regrading & Grading

Many commercial drainage problems originate from improper grading established during original construction or altered over time by additions, pavement changes, and soil settlement. Carolina Exterior performs site regrading to restore proper drainage patterns, directing surface water away from buildings, out of low spots, and toward appropriate collection or discharge points. Regrading projects range from targeted corrections of specific problem areas to comprehensive site drainage restoration across entire commercial properties.
Stormwater Management Solutions

Larger commercial properties and developments may be subject to local stormwater management requirements specifying how runoff must be managed on-site. Carolina Exterior installs stormwater management features including level spreaders, infiltration areas, and detention systems that manage peak discharge rates and meet local stormwater ordinance requirements. We coordinate with civil engineers and local municipality requirements where formal stormwater management plan compliance is needed.
Downspout & Roof Drainage Management

Building downspouts discharge concentrated volumes of water that can rapidly saturate soils adjacent to foundations and overwhelm surface drainage capacity. Carolina Exterior installs underground pipe systems, pop-up emitters, and dry creek bed features that capture downspout discharge and route it safely away from building foundations, parking areas, and landscape beds to appropriate discharge points.
Erosion Control & Stabilization

Slopes, embankments, and disturbed areas on commercial properties are highly susceptible to erosion that degrades appearance, damages neighboring properties, and creates sediment loading in stormwater systems. Carolina Exterior stabilizes eroded commercial slopes using appropriate combinations of grading, sod or seeding, erosion control blankets, retaining structures, and riprap armoring based on slope severity and water velocity.
Why Commercial Drainage Problems Must Be Fixed Quickly
Property owners and managers who defer drainage repairs often find that relatively modest problems escalate into significantly more expensive failures:

Liability Exposure: Wet parking lots and walkways create slip-and-fall hazards. Documented drainage problems that are not corrected represent known hazards with serious liability implications for property owners.
Property Damage: Chronic water intrusion adjacent to building foundations causes structural damage that is far more expensive to repair than the drainage system that would have prevented it.
Tenant & Customer Safety: Standing water, icy surfaces in winter (a consequence of drainage problems), and eroded walkways create conditions that drive tenant complaints and deter customers.

Regulatory Compliance: Local stormwater ordinances and property management covenants may require corrective action with defined timelines. Unaddressed drainage deficiencies can result in fines or compliance orders.
The Commercial Drainage Assessment Process
Carolina Exterior follows a structured three-step process for every commercial drainage project:

Site Assessment: We walk the property with you to observe drainage patterns, identify problem areas, evaluate existing drainage infrastructure, and assess contributing factors including grading, downspout routing, and paved surface conditions.

Diagnosis & Proposal: Based on the site assessment, we prepare a written diagnosis of drainage issues and a detailed proposal specifying recommended solutions, materials, installation approach, and project cost. Proposals are delivered within 5-7 business days of the site visit.

Installation: Commercial drainage installations are completed by experienced crews with minimal disruption to property operations. We coordinate staging and access to maintain safe, functional property use throughout the project and restore all disturbed surfaces upon completion.
Drainage Projects in Monroe, Waxhaw, Indian Trail & Charlotte

Carolina Exterior handles commercial drainage projects across Monroe NC, Indian Trail NC, Waxhaw NC, Weddington NC, Marvin NC, Wesley Chapel NC, Mineral Springs NC, Charlotte NC, Indian Land SC, and Lancaster SC. The clay-heavy soils common throughout Piedmont North Carolina create drainage challenges on many commercial properties, and our team has extensive experience diagnosing and solving drainage problems specific to local site conditions.
Frequently Asked Questions
How do I know if my commercial property has a drainage problem?

Clear indicators include standing water in parking lots or green spaces that persists more than 24 hours after rain, erosion visible on slopes or in landscape beds, water staining on building foundations at or below grade, tenant or customer complaints about wet conditions, and any visible damage to pavement or landscape areas attributable to water movement.
Do you handle stormwater management work?

Yes. Carolina Exterior installs stormwater management features including detention areas, level spreaders, and infiltration systems for commercial properties. For projects requiring a formal engineered stormwater management plan, we coordinate with the property's civil engineer to ensure our installation aligns with the approved plan. We can also refer qualified civil engineering firms for properties requiring new stormwater plan development.
Can drainage work be done while the property is operational?

Yes, in most cases. We sequence work to maintain access to building entries, parking areas, and loading docks throughout the project. For projects requiring temporary disruption to specific areas, we coordinate scheduling with property managers to minimize impact on tenants and customers and provide advance notice of any access limitations.
How long do commercial drainage installs take?

Timeline depends on scope. Targeted catch basin installations or single-zone French drain systems typically complete in 2-5 days. Larger regrading and drainage system projects range from 1-3 weeks. Comprehensive site drainage restoration for larger commercial properties may be phased over 4-8 weeks. Every proposal includes a specific project timeline.

Why HOAs in Waxhaw, Weddington & Marvin Trust Carolina Exterior

Union County's rapid residential growth has produced dozens of HOA communities across Waxhaw, Weddington, Marvin, Indian Trail, and Wesley Chapel, many of which have experienced inconsistent vendor service as demand for landscape contractors has outpaced supply. Carolina Exterior addresses this with local operational focus, stable crew assignments, and a client communication model designed specifically for HOA board governance requirements.

Photo documentation is particularly useful for HOA boards demonstrating active management to residents and for dispute resolution if questions arise about service completion.
What happens if there is storm damage to common areas?

Carolina Exterior responds to storm damage affecting HOA common areas with priority scheduling. We assess storm-related damage within 24-48 hours of a significant weather event affecting our service communities, provide the board with a written assessment and remediation proposal, and schedule cleanup and repair work as quickly as possible. Emergency storm response is available outside normal business hours for significant events.
Request an HOA Landscaping Proposal

Carolina Exterior provides free site assessments and detailed program proposals for HOA communities throughout Union County and the greater Charlotte region. We work with HOA management companies, board presidents, and property managers to develop proposals that meet board review and approval requirements.
